differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis (Class V) and secondary nephrotic syndrome
Antiphospholipid syndrome (APS) presenting with renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS) with hypercoagulable state
Bacterial pneumonia with secondary septic pulmonary emboli and reactive nephrotic range proteinuria
next_steps: Perform comprehensive serologic workup: ANA, dsDNA, complement levels (C3/C4), and antiphospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I)
Perform renal biopsy for definitive histological diagnosis (essential for guiding treatment)
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation for bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism, weighing bleeding risk
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending blood and sputum culture results
Evaluate for subacute endocarditis given the pulmonary and renal findings
Consult rheumatology and nephrology for urgent management of potential autoimmune disease
Quantify 24-hour urine protein or monitor spot protein/creatinine ratio trend

differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis (class V) and secondary nephrotic syndrome
Antiphospholipid syndrome (APS) presenting with renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Infective endocarditis with septic pulmonary emboli and immune-complex mediated glomerulonephritis
Primary membranous nephropathy with secondary hypercoagulable state
Renal vein thrombosis due to nephrotic syndrome (e.g., from underlying glomerulopathy)
next_steps: Perform comprehensive autoimmune panel: ANA, dsDNA, complement levels (C3, C4), and antiphospholipid antibodies (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I)
Obtain echocardiogram (TTE/TEE) to evaluate for valvular vegetations and right heart strain
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation, balancing bleeding risk with thromboembolic complications
Perform renal biopsy once the patient is hemodynamically stable and anticoagulation management is optimized
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending final blood and sputum culture results
Consult nephrology and rheumatology for management of potential systemic inflammatory/autoimmune disease
Perform quantitative assessment of urinary protein via 24-hour collection
